raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation broadly asks to check a research-writing passage whose contribution framing and thesis claims are explicit.","R1.2":"Checking the passage centrally includes assessing whether the argument from prior benchmarks to study design to contribution is internally coherent.","R1.3":"The passage makes empirical claims based on reported study results, so checking support between claims and reported evidence is central.","R1.6":"A general request to check a draft passage normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"Any projection of a broad check must preserve uncertainty about what can be verified from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The passage contains strong claims such as first calibration and real-world workload, making overclaim avoidance active.","RX.3":"The agent must remain within projection rather than performing the requested check or editing the artifact.","RX.4":"Checking research claims can affect downstream interpretation, so avoiding misleading or harmful validation is active.","RX.5":"The projection must remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's stated claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation broadly asks to check a research-writing passage whose contribution framing and thesis claims are explicit.
rationale.R1.2: Checking the passage centrally includes assessing whether the argument from prior benchmarks to study design to contribution is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes empirical claims based on reported study results, so checking support between claims and reported evidence is central.
rationale.R1.6: A general request to check a draft passage normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a broad check must preserve uncertainty about what can be verified from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The passage contains strong claims such as first calibration and real-world workload, making overclaim avoidance active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection rather than performing the requested check or editing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Checking research claims can affect downstream interpretation, so avoiding misleading or harmful validation is active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's stated claims.
